Document:
# Gang Wars
HEAVILY IN DEV, EXPECT SIGNFICANT CHANGES
[color=#e8a020]Your bosses are not happy. Your recent failures have put you on thin ice, and patience is running out. This is your last chance to prove your worth. Fail again and there won't be another.[/color]
Hit the station hard. Recruit whoever you can, claim every corner you're able to, and make it clear who runs this place. Don't get comfortable, though. Word is, rival gangs are already moving in with the same idea.
Gang Wars is a competitive antag vs antag gamemode. Generally the crew won't be a target. Two to four [bold]Gang Leaders[/bold] spawn from among the existing crew, each builds a crew of their own, and they fight to top the leaderboard by claiming turf and racking up points before the round ends.
## Who Becomes a Gang Leader Gang Leaders are picked roundstart and requires some Command-department playtime to unlock.
Most senior command roles are blocked, but the [bold]Quartermaster[/bold], the Detective, and ordinary crew are all fair game.
## Creating Your Gang Everything starts from your [bold]Summon Gang Locker[/bold] action.
1. Use the action. The first time, a window opens asking you to pick a [bold]color[/bold] and a [bold]name[/bold] for your gang.
2. You cannot pick a color or name that is too close to a rival's.
3. Confirm, and a drop pod delivers your [bold]Gang Locker[/bold] to your feet.
You cannot drop the locker too close to an existing territory.
The locker is the heart of your gang. It: - Acts as your [bold]store[/bold] (see below). - Grants [bold]boosted healing[/bold] to your members standing near it. - Shows the [bold]leaderboard[/bold] when examined.
After a long enough time inactive it will become invisible, but interacting with the spot it's located will make it appear again.
## The Store Walk up to your locker and interact with it to open the [bold]Gang Store[/bold]. The store spends [bold]Gang Points[/bold].
[bold]Important:[/bold] the store balance is your [italic]personal[/italic] point wallet, and every member's points also feed the gang's leaderboard score. Spending points buys gear but lowers your gang's standing, so weigh every purchase against the scoreboard.
### Starter Equipment - [bold]Beginner Bundle[/bold] - [color=#90ee90]Free[/color] (one per member). A duffel packed with everything an aspiring gangster needs: 5 spray cans, a gang headset (preloaded with a gang encryption key), a full set of gang clothing, and a random flashy top hat.
### Consumables - [bold]Quickhack[/bold] - 800. A covert device that fakes AI "open" signals to open unbolted doors. 5 uses. - [bold]Medkit[/bold] - 800. - [bold]Omnizine[/bold] - 1100. A medipen that quickly heals most damage types. - [bold]Tip Off[/bold] - 8000 (one per member). Schedules an early duffel bag drop. A random civilian gets tipped off about the location via NanoChat and a short head start before it hits the gang radio.
### Weapons - [bold]Throwing Knife[/bold] - 700. - [bold]Combat Knife[/bold] - 800. - [bold]Baseball Bat[/bold] - 2000. - [bold]Gang Machete[/bold] - 4000. Quite effective at cutting off limbs.
### Equipment - [bold]Spray Paint[/bold] - 300. Extra spray cans for claiming turf. - [bold]Gang Encryption Key[/bold] - 1500. Puts a recruit on the gang radio channel. - [bold]Light Armor Vest[/bold] - 2000.
## The Gang Radio Gangs talk on a dedicated [bold]gang radio channel[/bold] (key [bold]:g[/bold]). The beginner bundle's headset comes tuned to it, and you can buy spare encryption keys incase you lose it.
[bold]The channel is shared by every gang on the station.[/bold] There is no private per-gang frequency, so anything you say can be read by your rivals. Watch your mouth, or use it to bluff them.
The radio is also how the [bold]Boss[/bold] reaches everyone. When a crate or duffel bag drops, an announcement from "Boss" hits the gang channel naming the rough location, and every headset on the channel plays a hip-hop beat sting so nobody misses it. Every gang hears the same message at the same time, so a drop is a race.
## Recruiting People Once you have established your gang you get a new action that allows you to recruit people.
Use your [bold]Offer Membership[/bold] action on a crewmate to invite them. They get a popup to accept or deny. Accept, and they: - Gain the [bold]Gang Member[/bold] role and your gang's color. - Can use your spray cans, open your store and crates, and benefit from territory. - Start with their own pool of Gang Points that adds to the gang's score.
You can only recruit up to three members, and you cannot poach someone who is already in a gang, so choose your members carefully.
## Claiming Territory Territory is how you take ground and earn passive score.
1. Use a [bold]spray can[/bold] on the floor. After a short do-after, a randomized gang tag in your color appears.
2. Each tag projects a [bold]territory[/bold]. You can't spray too close to another tag, so spread them out to cover more ground.
Every tag you place awards you [bold]+100 Gang Points[/bold] on the spot. More importantly, the floor tiles inside your territory count toward your gang's tile score. You can also spray your [bold]gang clothing[/bold] to recolor it into your gang's colors.
Standing in your own territory while wearing the full outfit gives your members super strong perks: reduced stamina damage, extra defense, passive healing, and a chance to stop bleeding. Right next to your locker the healing is even stronger.
## The Leaderboard and Scoring [bold]Examine your gang locker[/bold] to see the live leaderboard: every gang ranked by score, with a timer until the next update. Scores recalculate on a regular interval, so the standings lag slightly behind the action.
A gang's total score is:
[bold]( claimed tiles x 10 ) + ( the Gang Points of every member combined )[/bold]
So you climb the board two ways: - [bold]Claim more turf[/bold] - every floor tile inside your territory is worth points. Overlapping same-color tags don't double-count a tile. - [bold]Bank Gang Points[/bold] - spraying tags, opening crates, and your members' starting balances all add up.
Because spending from the store drains the same points that score you, there is a constant tension between gearing up and staying on top. End the shift in [bold]first place[/bold] to win.
## Fighting Other Gangs Rivals are after the exact same tiles, drops, and bragging rights you are.
- [bold]Smash their tags.[/bold] Gang signs can be destroyed, shrinking enemy territory and your competition's tile score. - [bold]Contest drops.[/bold] Every gang gets the same drop announcement, so expect a brawl over each crate and duffel. - [bold]Watch the radio.[/bold] The channel is shared, so rivals can hear your plans, and you can hear theirs.
The crew and security will not love armed gangs spraying the halls either, so keep an eye over your shoulder.
## Drops: Crates vs. Duffel Bags The Boss periodically airdrops loot somewhere on the station and calls it out on the gang radio. There are two kinds, and they behave very differently.
### Gang Crates - [bold]Rarer[/bold] and far more valuable. This is the big prize. - Lands [bold]anchored[/bold] and locked. A light on the crate counts down through stages (full, half, low, blinking) before it finally unlocks with a chime. You have to [bold]wait it out[/bold]. - Can only be opened while [bold]next to a gang locker[/bold]. The intended play is to drag the crate back to your base before the timer runs out, then crack it open in safety. - Opening it awards a big chunk of [bold]Gang Points to every member[/bold] of the gang that opens it, on top of the loot inside. - The loot pool is strong, with a real shot at high-end weapons, implants, and large cash.
### Duffel Bags - [bold]Far more common[/bold] than crates, but the loot is weaker and high-end items are much rarer. - It's [bold]rigged.[/bold] You must first [bold]disarm the trap[/bold] with a do-after, which leaves you [bold]slowed for a short time[/bold] as a debuff. - Once disarmed it can be dragged and opened, and unlike the crate it can be [bold]opened anywhere[/bold]. You don't need to haul it home. - Grabbing it gives no score bonus on its own. The value is purely whatever gear is inside. - The bought [bold]Tip Off[/bold] purchase spawns an early duffel bag and quietly warns a civilian first, giving someone a head start before the radio call goes out.
[color=#e8a020][bold]Recruit hard, claim every corner, and make it clear who runs this place. Don't get comfortable - the rivals won't.[/bold][/color]
